messages.content: sigma.js initial color of the nodes and edges are black any solution?

To change the initial color of the nodes and edges in Sigma.js, you can use the following options:

1. For Nodes:
   - Use the `defaultNodeColor` option to set the initial color of the nodes.
   - For example, if you want to set the color to red, you can use: `defaultNodeColor: 'red'`.

2. For Edges:
   - Use the `defaultEdgeColor` option to set the initial color of the edges.
   - For example, if you want to set the color to blue, you can use: `defaultEdgeColor: 'blue'`.

Here is an example of how you can use these options when creating a Sigma.js instance:

```javascript
const s = new sigma({
  // ...other configuration options
  defaultNodeColor: 'red', // Set initial node color to red
  defaultEdgeColor: 'blue', // Set initial edge color to blue
});
```

Make sure to include these options when configuring your Sigma.js instance to change the initial color of the nodes and edges to your desired colors.
now the whole sigma gets blue
using above code I'm getting this error Uncaught TypeError: sigma.layouts is undefined

If you are getting the error "Uncaught TypeError: sigma.layouts is undefined", it means that the layout module of Sigma.js is not included or loaded correctly.

To resolve this issue, make sure you have included the layout module of Sigma.js in your HTML file before using any layout-related functions.

You can include the layout module by adding the following script tag in your HTML file:

```html
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/sigma.js/2.0.0/plugins/sigma.layout.fruchtermanReingold.min.js"></script>
```

Make sure to add this script tag after including the Sigma.js library script tag.

By adding this script tag, the necessary layout module will be loaded, and you should be able to use the Fruchterman-Reingold layout without the "sigma.layouts is undefined" error.
